game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **Glow Puzzle:** Glow Puzzle is a single-player line-drawing puzzle game where players must connect all given lines on a grid of dots without retracing any segment, forming a continuous Eulerian path. Players can choose between two control schemes: tapping a starting dot and then sequentially tapping adjacent dots to extend the line, or tapping and dragging a finger between adjacent dots to draw connections. The game features over 950 pre-designed puzzles, with progression occurring linearly as players successfully complete each puzzle to unlock the next. Difficulty scales by increasing the grid size, the number of dots, and the complexity of the required path, demanding more intricate logical deduction. The core loop involves solving individual puzzles, with success marked by completing the required path, and failure simply allowing a reset to attempt the puzzle again.
game_info.description: Connect all glowing dots on the grid by drawing a continuous path without lifting your finger or retracing any line segment. Each puzzle has exactly one solution - an Eulerian path that visits every connection once. Start at any dot and carefully plan your route. Complete increasingly complex puzzles to progress through all levels. Think ahead, as one wrong move can force you to restart the puzzle!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Navigate between dots on the grid
Space: Select/confirm dot and draw connection to adjacent dot
Shift: Undo last connection
Z: Reset current puzzle
Enter: Start game (from start screen)
Esc: Pause/unpause game
R: Return to start screen (from game over)

TEST_1.test_description: Basic movement and interaction testing. Validates that the player can navigate the grid, select dots, and create connections. Tests the fundamental mechanics of dot selection and path drawing. Ensures the game responds correctly to directional inputs and space bar presses.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Move randomly between adjacent dots using arrow keys. Periodically press space to create connections. Test undo functionality by pressing shift. Verify that invalid moves are blocked and valid moves update the game state correctly.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The test successfully navigates the grid, creates valid connections, and demonstrates that movement and selection mechanics work properly. Game state updates correctly with each action. No errors or crashes occur during random exploration.

TEST_2.test_description: Win condition testing using optimal path-finding algorithm. Implements a systematic approach to solve any valid Eulerian path puzzle. Uses graph theory principles to find a valid solution path. Tests if the game correctly recognizes completion and transitions to win state.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Analyze the puzzle structure to identify all required connections. Use a depth-first search with backtracking to find a valid Eulerian path. Navigate systematically through the grid, making connections in the optimal order. Complete the entire path without retracing any segment.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Successfully solves the puzzle by completing all required connections. Game transitions to GAME_OVER_WIN state. Score increases appropriately. Next puzzle becomes available. Validates that win conditions are properly implemented and detected.
